sources: Salesforce Description field (opp creation 2026-04-30): generic 'pain points' mention, no quantification
Open buyer commitment (2026-04-30): gather pain points from colleagues, not yet delivered
metrics.narrative: No quantified business case or financial impact has been articulated. The initial outreach on 2026-04-30 mentioned 'pain points in current procurement processes' (Salesforce Description field) but no specific cost, headcount waste, cycle-time drag, error rate, or savings target has been captured in any field or call. The deal has not progressed to a discovery call where metrics would typically be explored. The buyer has been asked to 'gather specific pain points from department colleagues' (commitment logged 2026-04-30) but that preparatory work has not been completed or shared. Without a quantified value case, there is no commercial anchor for the $150k amount or urgency for the close date. The highest-leverage move is to secure a discovery call and extract quantified pain: cost of manual supplier risk tracking, error rate in intake/requisitioning, cycle time for sourcing events, headcount dedicated to workarounds.

sources: OpportunityContactRole: Evan Bartholio, Business Systems Manager; Israel Powell, Manager, Supply Chain Operations (no Champion role assigned)
LastActivityDate 2026-05-05, no engagement since (64 days)
Rep-initiated Next Step 2026-06-22 with no buyer response
champion.narrative: No champion has been identified or developed. The two contact roles, Evan Bartholio (Business Systems Manager) and Israel Powell (Manager, Supply Chain Operations), are mid-level functional leads who expressed initial interest in April 2026 but have not demonstrated advocacy, access to the Economic Buyer, or internal selling behavior. Evan initiated the inquiry (raised the Govcloud question), which is a positive signal, but there has been no two-way engagement since 2026-05-05 (64 days), no introductions to senior stakeholders, no sharing of internal evaluation timelines or competitive intel, and no response to the rep's 2026-06-22 outreach. This is exploratory interest that has cooled, not championship. The deal is single-threaded through Evan and Israel with no executive access and no active internal advocacy. The immediate move is to re-engage Evan and Israel with the promised materials (Sourcing, SRM, Merlin Intake content plus the Govcloud answer), secure the discovery call, and during that call identify who among them (or another stakeholder) has the most to gain from solving the pain and can facilitate executive access.

sources: Salesforce Description (2026-04-30): 'pain points in current procurement processes' (status quo dissatisfaction)
Next Step 2026-06-22: focus on modules that extend/replace Infor ERP gaps
Competitors__c field: empty; no external vendor named in any source
competition.narrative: The primary competitor is status quo: Rocket Lab's current stack of Infor ERP plus manual processes and third-party bolt-on tools for supplier risk and sourcing workflows. The Salesforce Description (2026-04-30) references 'pain points in current procurement processes', implying dissatisfaction with the incumbent approach, and the requested focus on Sourcing, Supplier Risk & Performance Management, and Merlin Intake (Next Step 2026-06-22) maps to gaps Infor ERP does not natively fill. No named external competitors (Coupa, SAP Ariba, GEP, Ivalua, Jaggaer) appear in any field, task, or call note. The Competitors__c field is empty. The risk is that Rocket Lab is in research mode with no forcing function or budget allocated, making 'do nothing' the default outcome. The wedge is to quantify the cost of status quo (manual supplier risk tracking, lack of AI-driven intake intelligence, sourcing inefficiency) and position the Merlin agentic AI capabilities as step-change automation Infor cannot deliver, especially for aerospace supplier-risk complexity. Activation: make the Govcloud/compliance answer a door-opener, use the discovery call to quantify pain, and frame Merlin Intake as the Phase 1 quick win.

sources: Salesforce Description (2026-04-30): 'pain points in current procurement processes', no specifics
Open buyer commitment (2026-04-30): Evan Bartholio to gather pain points from colleagues, not delivered
identify_pain.narrative: Specific operational pain has not been articulated by the buyer. The Salesforce Description field (2026-04-30) contains a generic reference to 'pain points in current procurement processes' with no detail. The buyer (Evan Bartholio) was asked on 2026-04-30 to 'gather specific pain points from department colleagues who directly experience them, ahead of the discovery call', but this preparatory work has not been completed or shared, and the discovery call has not been scheduled. No call transcripts, tasks, or free-text fields name concrete pain: e.g., manual supplier scorecarding taking X hours/week, lack of real-time risk visibility causing compliance exposure, requisition approval bottlenecks adding Y days to cycle time, or Infor ERP gaps in sourcing/SRM forcing bolt-on tools. The pain owner is also unmapped; Israel Powell (Manager, Supply Chain Operations) is the likely pain owner for sourcing and supplier risk, but he has not articulated the pain on any call or in any captured interaction. The immediate move is to hold the discovery call and extract specific, quantified pain with a named owner.

sources: Explicit requirement (2026-04-30): Evan Bartholio asked whether platform capabilities are limited for Govcloud/government-contract customers
Overdue Zycus commitment (2026-04-30): confirm Govcloud functionality and report back (not delivered)
Current_Contract_Expiration__c: not populated
paper_process.narrative: Contracting and procurement mechanics are completely unmapped. No MSA, SOW, legal review, or InfoSec process has been discussed. The Govcloud compliance question raised by Evan Bartholio on 2026-04-30 is the only procurement-adjacent signal: he asked whether Zycus platform capabilities are limited for Govcloud/government-contract customers, and Zycus committed to confirm with the solutions consulting team, but that confirmation has not been delivered (overdue commitment). This suggests the buyer operates under government-contract constraints that will impose procurement-channel and hosting requirements, but the full paper process (legal review, compliance certifications, IT security review, ERP integration scoping with Infor) is unknown. Current_Contract_Expiration__c is not populated, so there is no forcing function from an incumbent contract end-date. The discovery call must surface: does Rocket Lab require Govcloud hosting, what certifications are required (FedRAMP, ITAR, etc.), who leads legal/InfoSec review, and is there an SI or Infor partnership angle for implementation.

sources: OpportunityContactRole: Israel Powell, Manager Supply Chain Operations; Evan Bartholio, Business Systems Manager (no EB/exec roles)
Salesforce Next Step 2026-06-22: no executive mentioned
No Tasks or Events reference an executive sponsor
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer or executive sponsor has been identified or engaged. The two contact roles are Israel Powell (Manager, Supply Chain Operations) and Evan Bartholio (Business Systems Manager), both mid-level functional leads with no budget authority signal. No calls, tasks, or free-text fields reference a CFO, VP of Supply Chain, VP of IT, or Procurement head. The Next Step (2026-06-22) references 'Prashant to follow up for a pre-demo call in June' but does not name an executive target. In aerospace/defense procurement (Rocket Lab's sector), spend authority for a $150k platform typically sits with a VP of Supply Chain or CFO, and government-contract compliance adds another approval layer. The deal cannot progress past demo without mapping and engaging the budget holder. The immediate move is to ask Israel Powell or Evan Bartholio who controls the procurement platform budget and to request an introduction.

sources: Salesforce What_is_the_decision_process__c: not populated
No Tasks, Events, or call notes reference approval workflow or selection process
decision_process.narrative: The buyer's decision process is unknown. No approval chain, vendor-selection methodology, or timeline milestones have been discussed or recorded. The two contacts are mid-level (Manager, Supply Chain Operations; Business Systems Manager), which suggests they are initiating exploratory research but are not the final decision-makers. In aerospace/defense with government contracts, procurement decisions typically require CFO or VP approval, IT/InfoSec review, and possibly compliance sign-off for Govcloud hosting. No fields (What_is_the_decision_process__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c, Multiple_approvals__c) are populated, and no calls have occurred to map the process. The discovery call is the forum to extract this: who evaluates, who shortlists, who approves budget, what gates exist (legal, InfoSec, IT architecture), and whether an RFP or multi-vendor bake-off is planned.

sources: Salesforce Products__c: iSource;iSupplier;eProcurement;Merlin;Merlin Intake;Agentic AI;ANA
Next Step 2026-06-22: focus on Sourcing, Supplier Risk & Performance Management, and Merlin Intake
decision_criteria.narrative: Product scope is documented (iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, ANA per Products field) and the Next Step specifies interest in 'Sourcing, Supplier Risk & Performance Management, and Merlin Intake', which implies evaluation criteria around sourcing workflow automation, supplier risk scoring/monitoring, and intelligent intake/requisition routing. However, no formal evaluation framework, RFP requirements, weighted scorecard, or test-case criteria have been shared. The deal is pre-discovery, so structured criteria have not yet been defined by the buyer. The partial signal is the module focus; the gap is the absence of decision weights, compliance requirements (beyond the Govcloud question), integration priorities, or a documented shoe-fit/BRD. The next milestone is the discovery call, where criteria should be extracted: must-haves vs nice-to-haves, compliance gates (Govcloud, ERP integration with Infor), and AI/Merlin appetite.

Sole champion candidate (Evan Bartholio) has not responded to follow-up in 65 days despite engaging well on 5/4 — no evidence of internal advocacy or access to power.

deal_momentum.text: Zero buyer touches in the last 30 or 60 days — last activity was the 5/4/26 intro call, 66 days ago, and no meeting is currently scheduled.
Next Step (6/22 entry) reads 'no material update' — a rep plan to send materials and chase a pre-demo call, not a dated buyer commitment.
Close date already slipped 35 days (Aug 20 → Sep 24) with no compensating buyer activity — a drifting, not advancing, timeline.
Outreach is one-way: rep sending Sourcing/SRM/Merlin materials into 65+ days of buyer silence on all channels.

The two calls that did happen (5/1 and 5/4, both recorded/transcribed) were substantive — buyer asked specific questions on ANA negotiation and AI-driven supplier risk — but that energy has gone cold since.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 2026-04-30, qualified same day, 69 days in Qualified stage with zero progression signals. Close date 2026-09-24 (78 days out) requires 10-11x the normal enterprise velocity from this stage. LastActivityDate 2026-05-05 (64 days ago); rep reached out 2026-06-22 with no buyer reply. Standard motion from Qualified to close is 12-15 months (discovery, demos, RFI/RFP, shortlist, workshops, commercials, negotiation, contracting); this deal has 78 days budgeted and has not yet held a discovery call. No submission-date milestones recorded. Forecast is Pipeline, which is appropriate, but the close date is fantasy.

Close date of 24 Sep 2026 (77 days out) is not credible given the deal remains at an exploratory Qualified stage with no discovery call yet completed, on a motion that typically runs 12-15 months (Zycus Deal-Progression Playbook).

deal_momentum.text: 65 days have passed since the last verified buyer activity (5 May 2026); the deal reads as cooling.
The only forward-motion event since the 4 May 2026 intro call is a one-way rep outreach logged 22 Jun 2026 with no buyer response.
The 4 May 2026 call ended with the buyer naming three priority modules and committing to gather internal pain points ahead of a discovery call.

customer_commitment.text: Evan Bartholio proactively named specific pain points (manual, ERP-reliant supplier risk tracking with no geopolitical/financial risk visibility; ad hoc contract management) unprompted on the 4 May 2026 call.
Buyer committed to gather colleague pain points and reconvene 'maybe late this week or next week' (from 4 May 2026), but no discovery call has materialized as of 9 Jul 2026, over 9 weeks later.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Product scope includes Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, and ANA, and the Next Step explicitly requests materials on Merlin Intake. However, no discovery call or demo has occurred, so the AI/Merlin value proposition has not been presented or tested with the buyer. No AIS score or status recorded, and no call transcripts exist to assess buyer appetite for AI-driven sourcing intelligence or intake automation. The deal is pre-discovery, so AI positioning is undefined, not under-positioned.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Product scope (iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI) aligns with stated interest in Sourcing, Supplier Risk & Performance Management, and intelligent intake. Govcloud/government-contract compliance requirement is unresolved and blocking; Zycus owes a confirmed answer.

hard.ais_why:
• Evan Bartholio joined as the Business Systems Engineer driving operations, supply chain, and AI initiatives. An explicit AI mandate from the prospect's own seat.
• Direct customer voice: the team is tired of just looking at reports and wants automation and AI to do the heavy lifting.
• Merlin Intake, autonomous negotiation, and supplier scorecards are all named areas of interest. Next steps scope Merlin Intake, supplier risk, and Teams integration follow-up.
